verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus meus attenuabitur ;  dies mei breviabuntur :  et solum mihi superest sepulchrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רוּחִ֣י חֻ֭בָּלָה יָמַ֥י נִזְעָ֗כוּ קְבָרִ֥ים לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My spirit shall be wasted, my days shall be shortened and only the grave remaineth for me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun henkeni on heikko, minun päiväni ovat lyhetyt, ja hauta on käsissä.
verses.king_james: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non peccavi,  et in amaritudinibus moratur oculus meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לֹ֣א הֲ֭תֻלִים עִמָּדִ֑י וּ֝בְהַמְּרוֹתָ֗ם תָּלַ֥ן עֵינִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have not sinned, and my eye abideth in bitterness.
verses.finnish_biblia: En minä ketäkään pettänyt, ja kuitenkin minun silmäni täytyy olla murheessa.
verses.king_james: Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?

verses.latin_vulgate: Libera me, Domine, et pone me juxta te,  et cujusvis manus pugnet contra me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִֽׂימָה־נָּ֭א עׇרְבֵ֣נִי עִמָּ֑ךְ מִ֥י ה֝֗וּא לְיָדִ֥י יִתָּקֵֽעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Deliver me, O Lord, and set me beside thee, and let any man's hand fight against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nyt siis taivuta sinus, ja ole itse minun takaukseni! kukas muu olis, joka minua takais?
verses.king_james: Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor eorum longe fecisti a disciplina :  propterea non exaltabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־לִ֭בָּם צָפַ֣נְתָּ מִשָּׂ֑כֶל עַל־כֵּ֝֗ן לֹ֣א תְרֹמֵֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hast set their heart far from understanding, therefore they shall not be exalted.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ymmärryksen olet sinä heidän sydämistänsä kätkenyt, sentähden et sinä korota heitä.
verses.king_james: For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prædam pollicetur sociis,  et oculi filiorum ejus deficient.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְ֭חֵלֶק יַגִּ֣יד רֵעִ֑ים וְעֵינֵ֖י בָנָ֣יו תִּכְלֶֽנָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He promiseth a prey to his companions, and the eyes of his children shall fail.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän kerskaa ystävillensä saaliistansa, mutta hänen lastensa silmät pitää vaipuman.
verses.king_james: He that speaketh flattery to his friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuit me quasi in proverbium vulgi,  et exemplum sum coram eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭הִצִּיגַנִי לִמְשֹׁ֣ל עַמִּ֑ים וְתֹ֖פֶת לְפָנִ֣ים אֶהְיֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath made me as it were a byword of the people, and I am an example before them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on minun pannut sananlaskuksi kansain sekaan, ja ääniksi heidän keskellensä.
verses.king_james: He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Caligavit ab indignatione oculus meus,  et membra mea quasi in nihilum redacta sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֣כַהּ מִכַּ֣עַשׂ עֵינִ֑י וִיצֻרַ֖י כַּצֵּ֣ל כֻּלָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: My eye is dim through indignation, and my limbs are brought as it were to nothing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun silmäni ovat pimenneet minun suruni tähden, ja kaikki minun jäseneni ovat niinkuin varjo.
verses.king_james: Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stupebunt justi super hoc,  et innocens contra hypocritam suscitabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָשֹׁ֣מּוּ יְשָׁרִ֣ים עַל־זֹ֑את וְ֝נָקִ֗י עַל־חָנֵ֥ף יִתְעֹרָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: The just shall be astonished at this, and the innocent shall be raised up against the hypocrite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tästä hurskaat hämmästyvät, ja viattomat asettavat heitänsä ulkokullatuita vastaan.
verses.king_james: Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tenebit justus viam suam,  et mundis manibus addet fortitudinem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֹאחֵ֣ז צַדִּ֣יק דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וּֽטְהׇר־יָ֝דַ֗יִם יֹסִ֥יף אֹֽמֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the just man shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hurskas pysyy tielIänsä: ja jolla on puhtaat kädet, se pysyy vahvana.
verses.king_james: The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur omnes vos convertimini, et venite,  et non inveniam in vobis ullum sapientem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם כֻּלָּ֣ם תָּ֭שֻׁבוּ וּבֹ֣אוּ נָ֑א וְלֹֽא־אֶמְצָ֖א בָכֶ֣ם חָכָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore be you all converted, and come, and I shall not find among you any wise man.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kääntäkäät siis teitänne kaikki ja tulkaat nyt: en minä kuitenkaan löydä yhtään taitavaa teidän seassanne.
verses.king_james: But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find one wise man among you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dies mei transierunt ;  cogitationes meæ dissipatæ sunt,  torquentes cor meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָמַ֣י עָ֭בְרוּ זִמֹּתַ֣י נִתְּק֑וּ מ֖וֹרָשֵׁ֣י לְבָבִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My days have passed away, my thoughts are dissipated, tormenting my heart.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun päiväni ovat kuluneet: minun aivoitukseni ovat hajoitetut, jotka minun sydämessäni olivat,
verses.king_james: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, even the thoughts of my heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noctem verterunt in diem,  et rursum post tenebras spero lucem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַ֭יְלָה לְי֣וֹם יָשִׂ֑ימוּ א֝֗וֹר קָר֥וֹב מִפְּנֵי־חֹֽשֶׁךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They have turned night into day, and after darkness I hope for light again.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ovat yöstä päivän, ja valkeuden pimeydestä tehneet.
verses.king_james: They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si sustinuero, infernus domus mea est,  et in tenebris stravi lectulum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֲ֭קַוֶּה שְׁא֣וֹל בֵּיתִ֑י בַּ֝חֹ֗שֶׁךְ רִפַּ֥דְתִּי יְצוּעָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I wait hell is my house, and I have made my bed in darkness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ehkä minä kauvan odottaisin, niin on kuitenkin hauta minun huoneeni, ja minä olen vuoteeni pimeydessä tehnyt.
verses.king_james: If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Putredini dixi : Pater meus es ;  Mater mea, et soror mea, vermibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַשַּׁ֣חַת קָ֭רָאתִי אָ֣בִי אָ֑תָּה אִמִּ֥י וַ֝אֲחֹתִ֗י לָרִמָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have said to rottenness: Thou art my father; to worms, my mother and my sister.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mätänemisen minä kutsuin isäkseni, ja madot äidikseni ja sisarekseni.
verses.king_james: I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi est ergo nunc præstolatio mea ?  et patientiam meam quis considerat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭אַיֵּה אֵפ֣וֹ תִקְוָתִ֑י וְ֝תִקְוָתִ֗י מִ֣י יְשׁוּרֶֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Where is now then my expectation, and who considereth my patience?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kussa on nyt minun odottamiseni? ja kuka ottaa minun toivostani vaarin?
verses.king_james: And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?

verses.latin_vulgate: In profundissimum infernum descendent omnia mea :  putasne saltem ibi erit requies mihi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּדֵּ֣י שְׁאֹ֣ל תֵּרַ֑דְנָה אִם־יַ֖חַד עַל־עָפָ֣ר נָֽחַת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: All that I have shall go down into the deepest pit: thinkest thou that there at least I shall have rest?
verses.finnish_biblia: Hautaan se menee, ja makaa minun kanssani mullassa.
verses.king_james: They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
